LAW AND MOTION 302, MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING OF 08/16/02 - MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE 11TH COA FOR UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ESTABLISH ANY UNFAIR PRACTICE THAT IS HARMFUL TO ANYONE BUT HIMSELF. THE MOTION IS DENIED IN ALL OTHER RESPECTS, AS TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT EXIST AS TO THE EXISTENCE OF TRADE SECRETS, WHETHER INTENTIONAL OR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATIONS WERE MADE AND RELIED UPON, AND BECAUSE PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL IS PROPERLY ALLEGED AS AN ALTERNATIVE TO BREACH OF CONTRACT. MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
Off Calendar Aug-22-2002 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: APPEAR: Rod Firoozye, Esq. 650/331-3888 for pltf & opposing party; Warrington S. Parker III, Esq. 213/624-7707 for defts & moving parties EarthLink, Inc. and Ranbir Chawla.
RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling of 08/16/02 - MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED AS TO THE 11TH COA FOR UNFAIR BUSINESS PRACTICES. PLAINTIFF DOES NOT ESTABLISH ANY UNFAIR PRACTICE THAT IS HARMFUL TO ANYONE BUT HIMSELF. THE MOTION IS DENIED IN ALL OTHER RESPECTS, AS TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT EXIST AS TO THE EXISTENCE OF TRADE SECRETS, WHETHER INTENTIONAL OR NEGLIGENT MISREPRESENTATIONS WERE MADE AND RELIED UPON, AND BECAUSE PROMISSORY ESTOPPEL IS PROPERLY ALLEGED AS AN ALTERNATIVE TO BREACH OF CONTRACT. Order signed in open court. (AJR/JH)
